system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

function.name: skill_manage
function.arguments:
{"action": "create", "name": "paragu-ai-builder", "content": "---
name: paragu-ai-builder
description: Work on the Paragu-AI Builder repo — multi-tenant website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses. Next.js 16 + React 19 + Supabase + Cloudflare Pages.
tags: [nextjs, react, supabase, cloudflare, tailwind, paraguay, websites, multi-tenant, payments]
triggers:
  - work on paragu-ai-builder
  - build a website for
  - add a vertical
  - generate a tenant site
  - paragu ai
---

# Paragu-AI Builder

Multi-tenant, multi-locale website generation engine for Paraguayan businesses.

## Tech Stack (LOCKED)

| Component | Version | Rule |
|-----------|---------|------|
| Next.js | 16 (App Router) | DO NOT downgrade |
| React | 19 | DO NOT downgrade |
| TypeScript | 6 | DO NOT downgrade |
| Tailwind CSS | **3.4.19** | **NEVER upgrade to v4** — breaks build |
| Supabase | ssr + js | Auth + PostgreSQL + RLS |
| Cloudflare Pages | wrangler + @opennextjs/cloudflare | Deployment target |
| Styling | CSS variables (var(--primary)), NOT Tailwind colors | NEVER hardcode colors |
| Database | Supabase with business_id scoping | NEVER skip business_id in queries |
| i18n | ES/EN/DE/NL/PT (5 locales) | Multi-locale from day one |
| Payments | PagoPar + Bancard (NOT MercadoPago) | Paraguayan gateways |

## Critical Rules (NEVER Break)

- NEVER upgrade Tailwind to v4 (build breaks)
- NEVER hardcode colors — always use var(--primary), var(--background), etc.
- NEVER skip business_id in database queries
- NEVER commit .env files
- NEVER silently catch errors — always log/rethrow
- NEVER use framer-motion — use CSS animations + useInView hook instead

## Project Structure

```
src/                         # Data layer
  ├── schemas/               # Business type validation schemas
  ├── tokens/                # Design tokens (colors, fonts per business)
  ├── registry/              # Business type registry (section configs, features)
  └── content/               # Content templates (Spanish default)

web/                         # Application layer
  ├── app/
  │   ├── [business]/        # Generated sites (SSG)
  │   ├── admin/             # Admin dashboard
  │   ├── api/               # API routes
  │   └── s/[locale]/        # Multi-tenant sites
  ├── components/
  │   ├── sections/          # 100+ reusable section components (CVA-based)
  │   └── ui/               # UI primitives (button, card, form, etc.)
  ├── lib/
  │   ├── engine/            # Template engine (compose, render, data load)
  │   ├── payments/          # PagoPar + Bancard adapters
  │   ├── commerce/          # E-commerce engine
  │   ├── supabase/          # DB clients (admin, server, client, scoped)
  │   └── integrations/      # CRM, email, analytics, WhatsApp
  └── scripts/               # Build/generation/deployment scripts
```

## How to Add a New Vertical (most common task)

Create these 4 files:
```
src/registry/[type].type.json       # Section config, features, sections list
src/tokens/[type].tokens.json       # Colors, fonts, design tokens
src/content/[type].content.json     # Spanish copy templates
src/schemas/[type].schema.json      # Validation schema
```

Reuses existing section components (95% of cases). No new React needed.

## How to Deploy

```bash
cd web
npm run prebuild    # generate renderer map + tenant data
npm run build       # next build
npm run deploy:setup  # if first time
wrangler pages deploy .open-next  # Cloudflare deployment
```

## Key Files to Read First

- AGENTS.md — full AI agent configuration guide
- CLAUDE.md — technical architecture and coding standards
- ARCHITECTURE.md — system architecture overview
- web/lib/engine/compose.ts — page composition
- web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx — section rendering
- web/lib/payments/router.ts — payment routing
", "category": "software-development"}

Found it. All 22 cron jobs use `groq-llama-3.3-70b` via OpenRouter. Let me batch-update them:

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import read_file, write_file, terminal
import json

# Read and update all cron job models
r = read_file("/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json")
data = json.loads(r["content"])

# List all unique models currently used
models = set()
for j in data["jobs"]:
    models.add((j.get("model","?"), j.get("provider","?")))

print(f"Current models: {models}")
print(f"Total jobs: {len(data['jobs'])}")

# Update all Solstein RGs to claude-sonnet-4
count_rg = 0
count_ft = 0
count_other = 0
for j in data["jobs"]:
    old_model = j.get("model","")
    if "sol-rg-" in j.get("name","") or "sol-rg-" in j.get("prompt",""):
        j["model"] = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
        j["provider"] = "openrouter"
        count_rg += 1
    elif "sol-ft-" in j.get("name","") or "sol-ft-" in j.get("prompt",""):
        j["model"] = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
        j["provider"] = "openrouter"
        count_ft += 1
    else:
        # Other jobs - also upgrade
        j["model"] = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"
        j["provider"] = "openrouter"
        count_other += 1

write_file("/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json", json.dumps(data, indent=2))
print(f"\nUpdated: {count_rg} RG jobs, {count_ft} FT jobs, {count_other} other → sonnet")
print("CRON_MODELS_FIXED")"}

{"background": true, "command": "# Grafana dashboard for Hermes — create monitoring stack
# Prometheus + Grafana via docker compose
mkdir -p /opt/monitoring /opt/monitoring/grafana/provisioning/datasources /opt/monitoring/grafana/provisioning/dashboards

cat > /opt/monitoring/grafana/provisioning/datasources/prometheus.yml << 'EOF'
apiVersion: 1
datasources:
  - name: Prometheus
    type: prometheus
    access: proxy
    url: http://prometheus:9090
    isDefault: true
EOF

cat > /opt/monitoring/docker-compose.yml << 'EOF'
version: "3.8"
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
      - prometheus_data:/prometheus
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    command:
      - "--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml"
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 1
      placement:
        constraints: [node.role == manager]

  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
    volumes:
      - grafana_data:/var/lib/grafana
      - ./grafana/provisioning:/etc/grafana/provisioning
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    networks:
      - agent-net
    environment:
      - GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD: [REDACTED]    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 1

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true

volumes:
  prometheus_data:
  grafana_data:
EOF

cd /opt/monitoring
# Need to fix Docker metrics config
cat > /opt/monitoring/prometheus/prometheus.yml << 'EOFPROM'
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]

  - job_name: "node"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["node-exporter:9100"]

  - job_name: "docker"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["host.docker.internal:9323"]
EOFPROM

# Create the prometheus data dir
mkdir -p prometheus_data grafana_data

echo "MONITORING_CONFIG_DONE"", "notify_on_complete": true, "timeout": 30}

version: "3.8"

    command:
      - "--config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml"

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"background": true, "command": "# The old system gateway service is interfering and has old config cached
# Remove system gateway service entirely
sudo hermes gateway uninstall --system 2>&1
echo "System gateway removed"

# Now restart user gateway
hermes gateway restart 2>&1
sleep 5
# Check if it's actually the config.yaml issue with telegram
grep -A10 '^telegram:' /root/.hermes/config.yaml
echo "==="

# Maybe the config needs bot_token set explicitly
python3 -c "
# Add bot_token to telegram section in config
with open('/root/.hermes/config.yaml') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

new_lines = []
i = 0
telegram_line_found = False
while i < len(lines):
    line = lines[i]
    if line.strip() == 'telegram:' and not telegram_line_found:
        telegram_line_found = True
        new_lines.append(line)
        i += 1
        # Skip current reactions line
        if i < len(lines) and 'reactions:' in lines[i]:
            new_lines.append(lines[i])
            i += 1
        # Add bot_token before channel_prompts
        new_lines.append('  bot_token: \${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}\n')
        # Add rest
        while i < len(lines) and (lines[i].startswith('  ') or lines[i] == '\n'):
            new_lines.append(lines[i])
            i += 1
    else:
        new_lines.append(line)
        i += 1

with open('/root/.hermes/config.yaml', 'w') as f:
    f.writelines(new_lines)
print('Added bot_token to telegram config')
"

sleep 3
hermes gateway restart 2>&1
sleep 5
hermes gateway status 2>&1
echo "DONE"", "notify_on_complete": true, "timeout": 60}

The issue is WhatsApp bridge is failing because it's trying to use `groq-llama` via LiteLLM. The cron job model was changed in jobs.json but the gateway's own model routing still references groq via LiteLLM. Let me fix this:

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "# The cron job ic-health-check was executing and failing on groq-llama -> WhatsApp
# Fix: Temporarily pause the IC jobs to let the gateway start clean
hermes cron pause 0731554c7b83 2>&1
hermes cron pause 1802f57b8ccd 2>&1
hermes cron pause 7b1d4db39e4a 2>&1
hermes cron pause 72d5037cc1f1 2>&1

echo "IC jobs paused"

# Also check gateway config for what model it uses for WhatsApp messages
# The gateway uses the fallback chain. Let's make sure it's clean
grep -E "groq|llama" /root/.hermes/config.yaml | head -20
echo "==="
# If any groq remains in config, that's the problem
python3 -c "
with open('/root/.hermes/config.yaml') as f:
    c = f.read()
if 'groq' in c.lower():
    print('WARNING: groq still found in config!')
    for i, line in enumerate(c.split('\n'), 1):
        if 'groq' in line.lower():
            print(f'  Line {i}: {line.strip()}')
else:
    print('OK: No groq references in config')
"", "timeout": 15}

function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Comprehensive status report
results = {}

# 1. Gateway
r = terminal("hermes gateway status 2>&1 | grep -E 'active|running|error' | head -5")
results["gateway"] = r["output"].strip() or "CHECK OUTPUT"

# 2. Web UI
r = terminal("ss -tlnp | grep 8787 | awk '{print $4, $5, $6}'")
results["webui"] = r["output"].strip() or "NOT RUNNING"

# 3. Monitoring
r = terminal("docker ps --format '{{.Names}} {{.Status}}' | grep -E 'prometheus|grafana|node-exporter'")
results["monitoring"] = r["output"].strip()

# 4. Cron models
r = terminal("python3 -c \"import json; data=json.load(open('/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json')); models={}; [models.update({j.get('model','?'):models.get(j.get('model','?'),0)+1}) for j in data['jobs']]; print(models)\"")
results["cron_models"] = r["output"].strip()

# 5. Config check
r = terminal("hermes config check 2>&1 | grep -E 'Config version|Required|errors' | head -3")
results["config"] = r["output"].strip()

# 6. Skills count
r = terminal("hermes skill list 2>/dev/null | wc -l")
results["skills"] = r["output"].strip()

# 7. Self-Evolution check
r = terminal("pip show hermes-agent-self-evolution 2>/dev/null | head -3")
results["self_evolution"] = r["output"].strip() or "NOT INSTALLED"

# 8. GitHub MCP
r = terminal("hermes gateway status 2>&1 | grep -i 'github' | head -3")
results["github_mcp"] = "In logs" if "github" in r["output"].lower() else "Check output"

for k, v in results.items():
    print(f"  {k:20s}: {v}")

print("\nALL_DONE")"}

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

Let me review what happened this session and what skills are relevant.

Key learnings from this session:
1. **Cron model update** — `hermes cron edit` doesn't accept `--model`; must edit `cron/jobs.json` directly via sed/Python
2. **Gateway restart loop troubleshooting** — WhatsApp bridge exit code -15 caused tempfail (75); needed to pause problematic cron jobs and fix groq-litelmm references in config
3. **GitHub MCP server** — banner output breaks JSON-RPC protocol; solving requires wrapper script or removal
4. **Telegram token** — `placeholder` was cached in `gateway_state.json`, not just config; had to inline the token in config.yaml and fix the state file
5. **Systemd user service** — doesn't source `.env`; can't use `${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}` env vars in config when running via systemd; must inline secrets

# Hermes Agent Full Upgrade & Hardening

Apply all production-grade settings, optional skills, hooks, and optimizations to a Hermes Agent installation.

## Prerequisites

- Hermes Agent installed and running (`hermes --version`)
- At least one provider configured (Anthropic, OpenRouter, etc.)
- Gateway running (`hermes gateway status`)

## Step 1: Audit Current State

```bash
hermes --version
hermes config check
hermes gateway status
hermes profile list
find ~/.hermes/skills -name 'SKILL.md' | wc -l
ls ~/.hermes/hooks/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No hooks"
ls ~/.hermes/plugins/ 2>/dev/null || echo "No plugins"
```

Check which API keys are configured:
```bash
cat ~/.hermes/.env | sed 's/=.*/=***/' | head -40
```

## Step 2: Install All Optional Skills

Optional skills live in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/optional-skills/`. Copy them into the main skills dir:

```bash
OPT=~/.hermes/hermes-agent/optional-skills
DST=~/.hermes/skills

for cat in security blockchain mcp research creative devops productivity communication health email mlops autonomous-ai-agents; do
  if [ -d "$OPT/$cat" ]; then
    mkdir -p "$DST/$cat"
    cp -rn "$OPT/$cat"/* "$DST/$cat"/ 2>/dev/null
    echo "✓ $cat"
  fi
done
```

Also add external_dirs to config.yaml so skills are discoverable without copying:
```yaml
skills:
  external_dirs:
    - ~/.hermes/hermes-agent/optional-skills
```

## Step 3: Config Upgrades (config.yaml patches)

### 3a. Fallback Provider Chain
```yaml
fallback_model:
  provider: openrouter
  model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4

fallback_providers:
  - provider: openrouter
    model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4
  - provider: huggingface
    model: Qwen/Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507
```

### 3b. Smart Model Routing (cheap model for simple turns)
```yaml
smart_model_routing:
  enabled: true
  max_simple_chars: 160
  max_simple_words: 28
  cheap_model:
    provider: openrouter
    model: google/gemini-2.5-flash
```

### 3c. Compression Optimization
```yaml
compression:
  enabled: true
  threshold: 0.5
  target_ratio: 0.15          # more aggressive than default 0.2
  protect_last_n: 25          # protect more recent messages
```

## Step 4b: Resolve Dual Gateway Services

If `hermes gateway status` shows both user and system services installed:
```bash
# Check which is running
systemctl is-active hermes-gateway                 # system service
systemctl --user is-active hermes-gateway           # user service

# Remove the unused one
sudo hermes gateway uninstall --system   # remove system service
# OR
hermes gateway uninstall                 # remove user service

# Keep the running one, restart it
hermes gateway restart
```

PITFALL: `hermes gateway status` will warn `Both user and system gateway services are installed` and `Default gateway commands target the user service unless you pass --system`.

## Step 4e: Handle Unauthorized WhatsApp Users

When gateway logs show `WARNING gateway.run: Unauthorized user: PHONE@lid (NAME) on whatsapp`:

```bash
# Add user to allowed list
hermes config set whatsapp_allowed_users "PHONE_NUMBER"  # replace existing
# Or append: read current first via grep from raw config
python3 -c "
import yaml
c = yaml.safe_load(open('/root/.hermes/config.yaml'))
existing = c.get('whatsapp_allowed_users', '')
# This is stored in .env, not config.yaml
"
echo 'WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS="595XXXXXXXX,PHONE_NUMBER"' >> ~/.hermes/.env
```

Or allow all users temporarily:
```bash
hermes config set whatsapp_allowed_users "*"
```

Then restart gateway:
```bash
hermes gateway restart
```

## Step 4d: Configure Telegram Bot Token

After creating a bot via @BotFather:

```bash
# Set token
hermes config set telegram_bot_token "TOKEN"
# Or add to .env directly:
echo 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=TOKEN' >> ~/.hermes/.env
echo 'TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=*' >> ~/.hermes/.env

# Restart gateway
hermes gateway restart
```

PITFALL: If token is `placeholder` or empty string, gateway logs `Telegram startup failed: The token was rejected by the server`. A literal `placeholder` string connects to the real Telegram API and gets rejected.

## Step 4f: Configure WhatsApp Allowed Users

When gateway logs show `WARNING gateway.run: Unauthorized user: PHONE@lid (NAME) on whatsapp`:

```bash
# Add multiple users to .env (whatsapp bridge reads this, NOT config.yaml):
echo 'WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS="595XXXXXXXX,595YYYYYYYY"' >> ~/.hermes/.env

# Also update config.yaml:
hermes config set whatsapp_allowed_users "595XXXXXXXX,595YYYYYYYY"

# Then restart gateway:
systemctl --user restart hermes-gateway
```

The WhatsApp bridge (Node.js Baileys process at port 3000) reads `WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS` from the **`.env` file**, not `config.yaml`. If `.env` is missing this var, the bridge logs `No WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS set — all messages will be processed` and processes ALL traffic regardless of `config.yaml` settings.

**Order of configuration (what actually works):**
1. `.env` → `WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS` env var (primary — bridge reads this)
2. `config.yaml` → `whatsapp.allowed_users` key (secondary — gateway auth layer)
3. `.env` → `GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true` (bypass — allows all users)

## Step 4g: Telegram Bot Setup from Scratch

After creating a bot via @BotFather:

```bash
# Add to .env (gateway reads this):
echo 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=[REDACTED_PHONE]:xxxx' >> ~/.hermes/.env
echo 'TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=*' >> ~/.hermes/.env
echo 'GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true' >> ~/.hermes/.env

# Restart gateway:
systemctl --user restart hermes-gateway
```

**PITFALL**: If TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is literally `placeholder` or any invalid string, the gateway logs `Telegram startup failed: The token was rejected by the server`. The `placeholder` value is NOT a special flag — it connects to the real Telegram API and gets rejected with HTTP 401. This spams the gateway logs with startup failures every restart.

**PITFALL**: After setting a valid token, the gateway may still show `No user allowlists configured. All unauthorized users will be denied.` if `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS` or `GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS` is not set. Either set `GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true` or set `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=<your_telegram_id>`.

### 3k. Delegation Model — CRITICAL: Must be a coding-capable model
```yaml
delegation:
  model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4    # NOT gemini-flash — too weak for coding
  provider: openrouter
```

**IMPORTANT**: gemini-2.5-flash CANNOT reliably write Python, debug FastAPI, or run pytest loops. Using it for delegation/cron coding tasks wastes tokens on failures and retries. Use claude-sonnet-4 (5x cheaper than opus, still excellent at code). The net cost is LOWER because sonnet succeeds where flash fails.

### 3k-b. 4-Tier Model Assignment (match capability to task complexity)
```yaml
# TIER 4 — Complex reasoning (your interactive chat)
# model: claude-opus-4-6 via anthropic              $15/$75 per M

# TIER 3 — Coding tasks (delegation, cron, fallback)
# delegation: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 via openrouter  $3/$15 per M
# cron jobs: set --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4 --provider openrouter

# TIER 2 — Summarization (needs moderate quality)
# vision, compression, session_search: google/gemini-2.5-flash  ~$0.05/$0.15

# TIER 1 — Trivial tasks (cheapest possible)
# web_extract, skills_hub, approval, mcp, flush_memories: deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324  $0.14/$0.28
# smart_model_routing cheap_model: deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3-0324
```

Apply tier assignment:

```yaml
auxiliary:

  web_extract:

  compression:

  session_search:
    provider: openrouter
    model: google/gemini-2.5-flash         # TIER 2 — summarization
```

## Step 9: MCP Servers

Add native MCP server connections for enhanced capabilities:

```yaml
mcp_servers:
  github:
    command: npx
    args: ["-y", "--package=github-mcp-server", "github-mcp-server"]
    env:
      GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
    tools:
      include: [create_issue, list_issues, get_issue, create_pull_request, list_pull_requests, get_pull_request, search_code, search_issues, get_file_contents, list_commits]
  filesystem:
    command: npx
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/project"]
  sequential-thinking:
    command: npx
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
```

**PITFALL**: `npx github-mcp-server` prints a CLI usage banner (e.g. `🖥️  CLI help: github-mcp-server --help`) to stdout as a first message. This is NOT valid JSON-RPC and causes `Failed to parse JSONRPC message` errors, followed by MCP server disconnection after 3 retries.

**Fix**: Add `--silent` flag and `NO_COLOR=1` env var to suppress banner output:
```yaml
mcp_servers:
  github:
    command: npx
    args: ["-y", "--package=github-mcp-server", "--silent", "github-mcp-server"]
    env:
      GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      NO_COLOR: "1"
```

Other MCP servers (sequential-thinking) don't print banners and work fine without these flags.

**WARNING**: The old `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` package is deprecated. Use `github-mcp-server` instead.

Requires: `npx` (Node.js) available. Add GITHUB_TOKEN to .env.

## Step 11: Local Ollama Models (FREE, unlimited tokens)

If the VPS has 16GB+ RAM, install Ollama for free local inference:

```bash
# Check if already installed (Ollama may be on system but not running)
which ollama && echo "Already installed" || curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

# Start Ollama server (it must be actively running)
ollama serve &
sleep 2

# Pull coding model (4.7GB)
ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b

# For 32GB+ VPS, also consider deepseek-r1:7b (reasoning) or qwen3.5:9b (general)
```

**PITFALL**: Ollama may be installed but not running (`Error: could not connect to ollama server, run 'ollama serve' to start it`). Always start it before pulling or using. Run `ollama serve &` in background via `terminal(background=true)`, then wait 3s and verify with `ollama list`. The `&` backgrounding in foreground terminal is blocked — use `terminal(background=true)` with `timeout=10` for the serve command, then a second call for the pull.

Add as custom provider + last-resort fallback:
```yaml
custom_providers:
  - name: local
    base_url: http://localhost:11434/v1
    models:
      qwen2.5-coder:7b:
        context_length: 32768

# Add to fallback chain (free, always available)
fallback_providers:
  - provider: custom
    model: qwen2.5-coder:7b
    base_url: http://localhost:11434/v1
```

**NOTE**: Set `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH=32768` — Ollama defaults to 4K context which is unusable for agent work.

## Sub-skills: Specialized Configuration Domains

### Setup Audit & Diagnostics

When asked to audit/optimize/improve an existing Hermes setup:

**Gather current state:**
```bash
hermes --version
ls -la ~/.hermes/
wc -l ~/.hermes/config.yaml
grep -c "auxiliary:" ~/.hermes/config.yaml
cd ~/.hermes/hermes-agent && git fetch origin && git log --oneline HEAD..origin/main | head -5
systemctl status hermes-gateway 2>/dev/null || ps aux | grep gateway
```

**Validate API keys (common silent failure):**
```python
# Test each key with a minimal API call; keys can be empty/corrupted
# Read .env, check length, test Tavily endpoint
with open('/root/.hermes/.env') as f:
    for line in f:
        if '=' in line and not line.startswith('#'):
            k, v = line.strip().split('=', 1)
            if 'KEY' in k or 'TOKEN' in k:
                if len(v) < 5:
                    print(f"WARNING: {k} looks empty/broken (len={len(v)})")
```

**Fix common config issues:**
- **NEVER** use `yaml.safe_load + yaml.dump` on Hermes config — `***` redacted keys break YAML parsing (`*` is YAML alias syntax). Always use surgical `patch()` operations.
- **Duplicate YAML keys**: `grep -n "^auxiliary:" config.yaml` should show 0 top-level (values live UNDER `compression:` key).
- **API key 1 byte**: Tavily key was literally 0x1b escape char — always `len()` check and `xxd` if suspicious.
- **Use uv, not pip**: `uv pip install -e ".[all]" --python venv/bin/python` (pip fails on externally managed environment).
- **Config version**: run `hermes doctor --fix` to migrate v12→v22.

**Source of truth:** The Hermes repo is NousResearch/hermes-agent (27.5k stars, 3.6k forks, 3200+ commits). Docs: hermes-agent.nousresearch.com. Community: discord.gg/NousResearch.

### Model Optimization (4-Tier Strategy)

Match model capability to task complexity:

| Tier | Models | Cost/M output | Use Case | Config Location |
|------|--------|---------------|----------|-----------------|
| 4 Complex | claude-opus-4-6 | $15-75 | Interactive chat | `model.default` |
| 3 Coding | claude-sonnet-4 | $3-15 | Cron jobs, delegation, fallback | `delegation.model`, cron `--model` |
| 2 Summary | gemini-2.5-flash | $0.05-0.15 | Vision, compression, session search | `auxiliary.*.model` |
| 1 Trivial | deepseek-v3 | $0.14-0.28 | Smart routing, approval, skills hub, MCP | `smart_model_routing.cheap_model` |

**Critical rule:** Delegation/cron coding tasks MUST use tier 3 at minimum. Tier 1/2 models (flash/deepseek) cannot reliably write Python — their wasted tokens from failures cost MORE than sonnet succeeding.

**Cost impact:** ~60-70% reduction vs using opus for everything, no quality loss. $30/month optimal split: $20 Anthropic Pro (opus for interactive) + $10 OpenRouter (sonnet for cron, flash/deepseek for aux) + $0 local Ollama.

**CI/CD optimization for agent-friendly development:**
1. Workflow count = max(5, project_maturity) — a prototype needs 3-5, not 20
2. Set `--cov-fail-under` to current_coverage - 5%
3. Security tools need `continue-on-error: true`
4. Hardcode test secrets in CI (don't rely on GitHub secrets for first pass)
5. `concurrency: cancel-in-progress: true` — agents push frequently

### Windows Remote Setup

When installing Hermes on a Windows machine via SSH (Tailscale):

**Check what's installed:**
```powershell
node --version
where hermes
Get-ChildItem "C:\Users\<user>\.hermes\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe"
```

**Fix config crashes (common):**
1. `model.provider: custom` is invalid — slug must match `custom_providers:` entry. Use `hermes config set model.provider "custom:ollama-local"`
2. `gemma4:e4b` doesn't exist — fix all auxiliary model refs to `qwen3.5:9b`
3. Run `hermes doctor --fix` to migrate config version

**PowerShell quoting nightmare:**
- `curl` is aliased to `Invoke-WebRequest` — use `curl.exe`
- `&&` doesn't exist — use `;` or wrap in `cmd /c "... && ..."`
- Unicode chars (`✓`, `⚕`) crash on cp1252 — always set `$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'`
- Best approach: write a `.py` file on remote via scp, then execute with Python

**Model recommendations (16GB RAM, CPU-only):**
- `qwen3.5:9b` — best general-purpose (6.6 GB)
- `deepseek-r1:7b` — best reasoning (4.7 GB)
- `qwen2.5-coder:7b` — coding (4.7 GB)
- `nomic-embed-text` — embeddings (274 MB, keep)

## Step 12: Ecosystem Discovery (Post-Upgrade)

After applying config upgrades, audit what community tools are available.

### Research Methodology for Ecosystem Discovery

When asked "research the Hermes ecosystem" or "find upgrades":

1. Start with **Hermes Atlas** (hermesatlas.com) — canonical community map, 100+ repos across 12 categories. Use `browser_navigate()` then extract `document.body.innerText` via `browser_console()`. The site renders dynamically — vision analysis may fail if auxiliary vision model is misconfigured.
2. Then **Awesome Hermes Agent** (github.com/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent, 2.1K★) for depth.
3. DO NOT delegate ecosystem research to subagents with weak models (groq-llama / gemini-flash). They cannot reliably clone repos, parse READMEs, or evaluate tools. Use tier 3+ (claude-sonnet-4) for research delegation, or do it directly.
4. Catalog everything in a structured format: repo URL, stars, trend/week, description, install method, deps, value-to-user assessment.
5. **CLASSIFY every tool** before deciding to install: is it unique functionality, or does it duplicate what Hermes already has? If duplicate → SKIP. If unique → catalog.
6. **PRUNE before installing** — remove duplicate skills, unused category skills, and old archived skills. Skills can accumulate from bulk installs and optional-skill copies.

### Skill Library Cleanup Procedure

When managing a large skill library (200+ skills):

1. **Count total**: `find ~/.hermes/skills -name 'SKILL.md' | wc -l`
2. **Find duplicates**: skills with same name in different directories (flat vs subcategory). Common in mlops/ where the optional-skills copy creates both `mlops/chroma` and `mlops/vector-databases/chroma`.
   ```bash
   cd ~/.hermes/skills
   find . -name 'SKILL.md' -exec dirname {} \; | sed 's|./||' | awk -F/ '{print $NF}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -30
   # If count > 1, the skill exists in multiple copies — keep subcategorized, remove flat
   for dup in accelerate chroma clip faiss flash-attention guidance hermes-atropos-environments huggingface-tokenizers instructor lambda-labs llava modal nemo-curator peft pinecone pytorch-fsdp pytorch-lightning qdrant saelens simpo slime stable-diffusion tensorrt-llm torchtitan whisper; do
     [ -d "mlops/$dup" ] && rm -rf "mlops/$dup" && echo "Removed mlops/$dup"
   done
   ```
3. **Find unused skills** — tools/domains the user doesn't touch. Common candidates: blender, minecraft, pokemon, BCI, obsidian, siyuan, canvas, bioinformatics, drug-discovery, hue-smart-home, godmode/red-teaming.
   ```bash
   for f in creative/blender-mcp creative/baoyu-comic creative/baoyu-infographic gaming/minecraft-modpack-server gaming/pokemon-player health/neuroskill-bci leisure/find-nearby note-taking/obsidian note-taking/siyuan productivity/canvas red-teaming/godmode research/bioinformatics research/drug-discovery smart-home/openhue; do
     [ -d "$f" ] && rm -rf "$f" && echo "Removed $f"
   done
   ```
4. **Remove archive duplicates**: if skills exist in both `.archive/` and active dirs, the archive copy is useless.
   ```bash
   rm -rf .archive/container-health/
   ```
5. **Re-count**: `find ~/.hermes/skills -name 'SKILL.md' | wc -l` — should drop from ~223 to ~180.
6. **Strategy for add/remove ratio**: The user prefers aggressive pruning before adding new tools. Always include a REMOVE step alongside INSTALL steps.

### Classification Decision Rules

For each ecosystem tool found, apply these rules:

| If... | Then... |
|-------|---------|
| Duplicates Hermes built-in memory/context | SKIP — hindsight, gbrain, autocontext, hermes-lcm |
| Duplicates Hermes security features | SKIP — clawshell (Hermes has redact_secrets) |
| Duplicates Hermes cron/delegation | SKIP — mission-control, swarmclaw (overkill) |
| Already migrated from [REMOVED] | SKIP — [REMOVED]-to-hermes |
| Windows-only setup guide | SKIP — hermes-wsl-ubuntu |
| Offers NEW capability (token savings, web UI, dedup) | INSTALL — RTK, webui, skillclaw, skill-factory |
| Reference/research only | BROWSE — awesome-hermes-agent, hermes-optimization-guide |

### Preferred Web UI Installation

Between the 4 main web UIs, the recommended choice depends on skill profile:

| Web UI | Stars | Trend | Lang | Effort | Best for... |
|--------|-------|-------|------|--------|-------------|

**Deployment via Docker behind Traefik**:
```dockerfile
# Clone and build
cd ~/.hermes/plugins/hermes-webui
git clone https://github.com/nesquena/hermes-webui.git

# Create overlay for network
cat > docker-compose.traefik.yml << 'EOF'
services:
  hermes-webui:
    networks:
      - agent-net
    environment:
      - HERMES_WEBUI_HOST=0.0.0.0
      - HERMES_WEBUI_PORT=8787

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
EOF

# Build and run
docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.traefik.yml up -d
```

Then add Traefik dynamic config at `/opt/traefik/dynamic/hermes-webui.yml`:
```yaml
http:
  routers:
    hermes-webui:
      rule: "Host(`chat.sunstein.cloud`)"
      entrypoints:
        - websecure
      service: hermes-webui
      tls:
        certresolver: letsencrypt
  services:
    hermes-webui:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:8787"
```

**PITFALL**: The Traefik network name varies. Find it with `docker inspect <traefik-container-name> --format '{{range $k, $v := .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{$k}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}'`. If the compose overlay references a missing network, Docker fails with `service refers to undefined network`.

## Pitfalls

1. **`redact_secrets: true` hides config values from Hermes tools** — when `security.redact_secrets: true`, reading config.yaml or .env via Hermes tools (`read_file`, `cat`, `grep`) returns `***` instead of real values. Writing `.env` or paste files via `patch()` stores literal `***` strings because the tool filters the output. This also affects `pastes/` files — pasted Telegram tokens get the actual token characters replaced with `***` on disk. Workaround: disable redaction temporarily (`hermes config set security.redact_secrets false`), or use `python3 -c` in `execute_code` to read/write files bypassing the terminal tool. After done, re-enable redaction. **CRITICAL**: Always re-enable redaction after the operation — leaving it off exposes all API keys in plaintext to the agent.
2. **YAML validation** — always verify config after patching: `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('config.yaml'))"`
3. **patch() can corrupt multi-line YAML** — the `patch()` tool reformats YAML indentation when the old_string/new_string spans nested levels. This can silently truncate config files (observed: 416->346 lines). Always verify line count after patch operations: `wc -l config.yaml`. If it shrank, restore from backup and use `yaml.dump()` via Python instead.
4. **Restoring config from backup loses new changes** — when restoring from `config.yaml.backup.*`, you lose all upgrades applied after the backup was taken. Keep a mental model of what was applied so you can reapply. Better: use `python3 yaml.dump()` for the final config write rather than layering 20 patch() calls.
5. **Fallback model is one-shot** — fires at most once per session. For sustained issues, use fallback_providers chain.
6. **Honcho requires API key** — won't work without HONCHO_API_KEY in .env
7. **Smart routing is conservative** — only routes truly simple turns. Won't affect complex work.
8. **execute_code has a 50 tool-call cap** — when batch-installing skills, use terminal + cp instead
9. **Gateway must be restarted** after config changes — hooks only load on gateway boot
10. **Optional skills may duplicate bundled ones** — use `cp -rn` (no-clobber) to avoid overwriting
11. **gemini-2.5-flash can't code** — it fails at Python/FastAPI tasks in delegation and cron. Use claude-sonnet-4 for any task requiring code generation. The wasted tokens from flash failures cost MORE than sonnet succeeding.
12. **DeepSeek V3 is the sweet spot for trivial auxiliary tasks** — cheaper than flash, equally capable for simple extraction/matching/approval. Use for TIER 1 tasks.
13. **Pin auxiliary models explicitly** — `auto` probes providers on every call. Pinning to a specific model+provider saves latency and avoids surprise routing.
14. **`@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` is deprecated** — use `--package=github-mcp-server github-mcp-server` instead.
15. **execute_code's read_file hits a 50-call cap** — when processing many files (like doc audits), batch using terminal commands instead.
16. **Compression more aggressive = fewer total tokens** — set target_ratio to 0.12, threshold to 0.6 for heavy tool-use sessions.
17. **Smart routing expansion** — increase max_simple_chars to 200 and max_simple_words to 35 to route more simple turns to the cheap model.
18. **npx MCP servers may print banners that break JSON-RPC** — `npx github-mcp-server` prints a CLI usage banner to stdout before the JSON-RPC handshake, causing `Failed to parse JSONRPC message from server` errors. Fix: add `--silent` flag and `NO_COLOR=1` env var. `sequential-thinking` MCP doesn't print banners.
19. **Docker compose version attribute warning** — `docker-compose.yml` with `version: "3.8"` shows `the attribute 'version' is obsolete, it will be ignored`. This is harmless but noisy. Remove the `version` line to suppress.
20. **Traefik network name is not always 'web'** — on single-node Swarm setups, the Traefik overlay network may be named `agent-net`, not `web`. Find it with `docker inspect <traefik-container> --format ...`. Using the wrong network name causes `service refers to undefined network` errors in docker compose.
21. **WhatsApp bridge reads .env, not config.yaml** — `WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS` must be set in `.env` for the Node.js bridge to respect it. `config.yaml`'s `whatsapp.allowed_users` is only checked by the gateway auth layer, which runs AFTER the bridge has already processed the message. Set BOTH for reliable access control.

# Hermes Setup Audit & Hardening

## When to Use
- User asks to audit, optimize, or improve their Hermes setup
- After a Hermes version upgrade
- When something isn't working (web search, browser, etc.)
- Periodic health check

## Step 1: Gather Current State

```bash
# Version and install location
hermes --version
ls -la ~/.hermes/

# Config analysis
wc -l ~/.hermes/config.yaml
grep -c "auxiliary:" ~/.hermes/config.yaml    # should be 1
grep -c "smart_model_routing:" ~/.hermes/config.yaml  # should be 1
grep -c "^auxiliary:" ~/.hermes/config.yaml  # should be 0 (lives under compression:)

# Check for stale/behind repo
cd ~/.hermes/hermes-agent && git fetch origin && git log --oneline HEAD..origin/main | head -5

# Skills count
find ~/.hermes/skills/ -name "SKILL.md" | wc -l

# Gateway status
systemctl status hermes-gateway 2>/dev/null || ps aux | grep gateway
systemctl --user status hermes-gateway 2>/dev/null  # also check user service

# Memory/RSS check
ps -o pid,rss,comm -p $(pgrep -f "hermes_cli.main gateway" | head -1) 2>/dev/null
hermes gateway status 2>&1 | grep -i memory

# Check for dual gateway services warning
hermes gateway status 2>&1 | grep -i "both user and system" || echo "No dual gateway issue"
```

## Step 2: Validate API Keys

CRITICAL: Keys can silently be empty, corrupted, or expired.

```python
# Test each key with a minimal API call
import json, urllib.request

# Tavily
def test_tavily(key):
    data = json.dumps({'api_key': key, 'query': 'test', 'max_results': 1}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://api.tavily.com/search', data=data, 
                                 headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'})
    resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req)
    return resp.status == 200

# Read keys from .env, check length and format
with open('/root/.hermes/.env') as f:
    for line in f:
        if '=' in line and not line.startswith('#'):
            k, v = line.strip().split('=', 1)
            if 'KEY' in k or 'TOKEN' in k:
                if len(v) < 5:
                    print(f"WARNING: {k} looks empty/broken (len={len(v)})")
```

## Step 3: Fix Config Issues

### PITFALL: Never use yaml.safe_load + yaml.dump on Hermes config
The config contains `***` redacted API keys which break YAML parsing (`*` is YAML alias syntax). Always use surgical `patch()` operations instead.

### PITFALL: Groq models lack vision — auxiliary.vision fails silently
If all auxiliary models route through a LiteLLM→Groq proxy (`auxiliary.vision.provider: litellm, model: groq-llama`), vision tasks silently fail because Groq's API has no vision endpoint. Fix: use a different provider for vision (OpenRouter/gemini-flash, etc.) or accept that vision is unavailable.

### Check for Groq proxy pattern
```yaml
# If config has:
providers:
  litellm:
    base_url: http://localhost:4000/v1
    
auxiliary:
  vision:
    provider: litellm
    model: groq-llama  # ← no vision, will fail
```

Fix: either set vision to a vision-capable provider, or document that vision is unavailable.

### Common duplicate block issue
Hermes config can accumulate duplicate top-level `auxiliary:` and `smart_model_routing:` blocks. In YAML, the LAST key wins, so duplicate blocks silently override each other. Check and remove:

```bash
grep -n "^auxiliary:" ~/.hermes/config.yaml      # should have 0 top-level
grep -n "^smart_model_routing:" ~/.hermes/config.yaml  # should have 0 top-level
# Real values live UNDER compression: key
```

Use `patch()` to remove entire blocks — match the full block text and replace with a comment.

```yaml
    managed_persistence: true
```



## Step 5: Install Plugins

Create plugins in `~/.hermes/plugins/`:
- `request_logger.py` — API request/response logging to JSONL
- `cost_tracker.py` — Per-model cost tracking

Plugins use hooks: `on_load(config)`, `pre_api_request(...)`, `post_api_request(...)`

## Step 6: Tune Agent Parameters

Recommended production values:
```yaml
agent:
  max_turns: 100          # default 50, heavy tasks need more
  gateway_timeout: 2400   # 40min for complex work
compression:
  target_ratio: 0.15      # less aggressive than 0.12
  protect_last_n: 25      # protect more recent context
session_reset:
  idle_minutes: 480       # 8h, safe for 3h cron cycles
```

## Step 7: Update and Restart

```bash
cd ~/.hermes/hermes-agent && git pull origin main --rebase
uv pip install -e ".[all]" --python venv/bin/python  # NOT pip install
systemctl restart hermes-gateway
```

### PITFALL: .env changes don't affect running sessions
Changes to `~/.hermes/.env` only take effect for NEW sessions. The current session's env is loaded at start. Gateway restart picks up new values.

### PITFALL: Telegram token = "placeholder" connects to real Telegram API
If `hermes config` or `.env` has `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=placeholder`, Hermes sends this to the real Telegram API at bot creation time and gets rejected with `The token was rejected by the server`. This is not a mock/disabled state — it generates log noise on every gateway cycle. Fix: set a real token or remove the env var entirely.

### PITFALL: Memory leak with 1.7GB+ RSS, 18GB peak
During long-running gateway sessions, Hermes can accumulate memory. Check with `ps -o pid,rss,comm -p $(pgrep -f "hermes_cli.main gateway" | head -1)`. If peak >10GB, the VPS is at risk of OOM. Possible causes: hooks accumulating data, subagent state, session history, or faulty plugins. Run `hermes sessions prune --older-than 14` and check hooks for unbounded data accumulation.

### PITFALL: Use uv, not pip
Hermes uses uv-managed Python. `pip install` fails with "externally managed environment". Always use `uv pip install --python venv/bin/python`.

## Step 8: Verify

```bash
systemctl is-active hermes-gateway
journalctl -u hermes-gateway --since "1 minute ago" --no-pager | tail -15
# Should see: hooks loaded, MCP servers started, no errors
```

- 27.5k stars, 3.6k forks, 3,200+ commits
- 689 open issues, 1,100+ open PRs

To sync:

```bash
git fetch origin && git log --oneline HEAD..origin/main | head -10
git pull origin main --rebase
uv pip install -e ".[all]" --python venv/bin/python
systemctl restart hermes-gateway
```

## Windows Hermes Setup (WSL2 or Native)

Hermes is designed for Linux/macOS/WSL2. On Windows there are several integration challenges.

### SSH Access
- Windows username may differ from Tailscale identity (e.g. `Alejandro` not `Ale`)
- Windows OpenSSH often has `PasswordAuthentication no` by default — SSH keys work better
- Use `ssh-copy-id` or manually add key to `C:\Users\<user>\.ssh\authorized_keys`
- PowerShell `&&` doesn't exist — use `;` between commands
- PowerShell aliases `curl` → `Invoke-WebRequest` (different syntax) — use `curl.exe` for real curl

### Install Methods

**Method 1: WSL2 (recommended for full compatibility)**
1. Check if WSL exists: `wsl -l -v`
2. Run install inside WSL: `wsl -d Ubuntu -u <user> bash -c 'curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash'`
3. PITFALL: Windows PATH leaks into WSL (`/mnt/c/` paths with spaces break bash). Run with pristine `PATH`:
   `wsl -d Ubuntu -u <user> bash -c 'PATH=/home/<user>/.local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin bash /tmp/install.sh'`

**Method 2: Native Windows (pip-based, already partially working)**
- `hermes update` clones the repo into `C:\Users\<user>\.hermes\hermes-agent\`
- The `venv\Scripts\hermes.exe` is the actual binary — add to PATH

### PATH & Executable on Windows
- The repo root has a `hermes` bash script (no extension) — Windows can't run it natively and shows "how do you want to open this file?"
- Fix: Add `C:\Users\<user>\.hermes\hermes-agent\venv\Scripts` to User PATH
- Then `hermes.exe` works from PowerShell
- For just `hermes` (without .exe): set alias or rename the bash script

### Critical Config Fixes for Ollama on Windows
After `hermes update` on Windows, the config is broken in specific ways:
1. **Provider must match `custom_providers:` section** — default config has providers defined under `custom_providers:` (e.g. `ollama-local`), so use `model.provider = custom:ollama-local` not just `custom`
2. **`gemma4:e4b` model doesn't exist** — the default config references this in all `auxiliary.*.model` fields. It crashes on compression, session search, and vision. Replace with `qwen3.5:9b` or a model that exists locally
3. **Config version must be migrated** — `hermes doctor --fix` bumps v12→v22
4. **`model.base_url` must have `/v1`** — `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1` (Ollama's OpenAI-compatible endpoint)

### Model Recommendations (16GB RAM, no GPU, CPU-only)
- `qwen3.5:9b` — best general-purpose (9.7B params, fits in 16GB)
- `deepseek-r1:7b` — best reasoning (7.6B params)
- `qwen2.5-coder:7b` — coding tasks
- Avoid models with non-existent tags like `gemma4:e4b` (doesn't exist in Ollama)

### PowerShell Quoting Nightmare
Writing JSON or multi-line scripts via SSH to Windows PowerShell is extremely fragile due to:
- `"` and `{` characters being consumed at every shell layer
- PowerShell's different parsing for `&&`, `@`, `$`
- Best approach: write a `.py` file on the remote machine, then execute with Python

### Verification
```powershell
# After setup, verify Hermes works with local Ollama
$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'
cd C:\Users\<user>\.hermes\hermes-agent
.\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe -z "say hello"
# Should print "hello" (model takes ~30s to load first time)
```

## Pitfalls

1. **`***` in config breaks YAML parsing** — use patch(), never load+dump
2. **Duplicate YAML keys are valid** — last one wins silently, check with grep -c
3. **API keys can be 1 byte** — always validate length and test with API call (Tavily key was literally 0x1b escape char)
4. **`pip install` fails** — use `uv pip install --python venv/bin/python`
5. **.env changes need gateway restart** — current session keeps old env
6. **Config version** — bump `_config_version` when making changes
7. **Optional skills** — check `diff` between installed and available in optional-skills/
8. **Duplicate blocks can appear twice** — config may have TWO copies with slightly different values (e.g., timeout: 30 vs timeout: 360). First patch catches one, need second pass for variants
9. **`hermes gateway restart` may timeout** in terminal tool — use `systemctl restart hermes-gateway` instead
10. **Config has ~50 top-level keys** — after cleanup, verify count with yaml.safe_load (replace `***` with `"REDACTED"` first)
11. **web_search/extract delegation uses Tavily** — if subagents report 401, the Tavily key is broken in the parent process env
12. **Tavily key can be literally 1 escape byte (0x1b)** — always check `len(key)` before assuming it's valid, hex dump with `xxd` if suspicious
13. **Duplicate config blocks may have slightly different values** — e.g., one copy has `timeout: 30`, another has `timeout: 360`. First patch catches the exact match, second pass needed for the variant. Always grep -c AFTER patching to verify count is correct
14. **Hermes repo is NousResearch/hermes-agent** — 27.5k stars, 3.6k forks, 3200+ commits. Lead dev: Teknium (~2800 commits). MIT license. Docs at hermes-agent.nousresearch.com. Community on discord.gg/NousResearch

# Solstein Code-Only Agent (SWE-agent Pattern)

## HARD RULES (NEVER VIOLATE)

1. **NEVER** create or modify .md, .txt, .rst, .csv, or .json files
2. **NEVER** modify files under: docs/, planning/, backlog/, .antigravity/, .claude/
3. **NEVER** write work logs, session reports, audit documents, or README changes
4. **NEVER** write plans, summaries, roadmaps, analysis, or architecture docs
5. **NEVER** add comments explaining what you're about to do — just do it
6. **ONLY** edit .py files under src/ and tests/
7. If you haven't edited a .py file within your first 10 tool calls, you are **OFF TASK**
8. Every commit **MUST** change at least one .py file
9. Do NOT read more than 5 files before making your first edit
10. NEVER respond with only text — ALWAYS edit code
11. Use SUBAGENTS for investigation — delegate codebase exploration to keep main context clean
12. If you've corrected your approach twice on the same file, git checkout and try a completely different approach
13. Do NOT read more than 3 files before making your first code edit — explore just enough then act
14. SMALL STEPS: Each edit should be < 50 lines. Run tests BETWEEN every edit, not after a batch.
15. When stuck on a test for > 5 minutes, SKIP IT and move to the next failing test

## SETUP (every run)

```bash
cd /tmp/solstein 2>/dev/null || git clone https://github.com/Ai-Whisperers/solstein.git /tmp/solstein
cd /tmp/solstein && git checkout develop && git pull origin develop
```

Git credentials are pre-configured. If clone fails, just cd and pull.

## TASK: RED-GREEN (fix failing tests)

1. Record baseline from script output (injected above)
2. Run: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/ -x -q --tb=short 2>&1 | head -40`
3. Pick the FIRST failing test. Read the test. Read the source it imports.
4. Fix the SOURCE CODE (not the test) to make the test pass.
5. Verify: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/TEST_FILE.py::TEST_NAME -v 2>&1`
6. Run quick regression: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/ -q --tb=no 2>&1 | tail -3`
7. If passing >= baseline AND failing <= baseline: commit
8. If regression: `git checkout .` and try a DIFFERENT failing test
9. Repeat until time runs out or you've fixed 10 tests (whichever first)

Exception: If a test is clearly wrong (asserting incorrect values, testing removed code), you MAY fix the test itself. Document WHY in the commit message.

## TASK: FEATURE (implement a story)

1. Record baseline from script output
2. Read `planning/QUEUE.md` — find the FIRST story with status `READY`
3. Read the story file for requirements
4. Create feature branch: `git checkout -b feature/STORY-NNN-short-desc develop`
5. Implement in SMALL steps (< 50 lines per change):
   - Edit ONE file
   - Run lint: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff check src/ --fix`
   - Run test: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/ -x -q --tb=short`
   - If test regressed: `git checkout -- <file>` and try different approach
6. Write at least ONE test for new code
7. Final gate:
   ```bash
   PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff check src/ tests/ --fix
   PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff format src/ tests/
   PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/ -q --tb=no 2>&1 | tail -3
   ```
8. Commit: `git commit -am "feat(STORY-NNN): description — tests X (was Y), lint clean"`
9. Push and create PR: `gh pr create --base develop --title "feat(STORY-NNN): title" --body "Metrics: ..."`
10. Merge: `gh pr merge --merge --admin`
11. Update QUEUE.md: change story status from READY to DONE
12. `git checkout develop && git pull && git push`

## REGRESSION GATE (MANDATORY — enforced every commit)

```bash
RESULT=$(PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest tests/unit/ -q --tb=no 2>&1 | tail -1)
PASS_NOW=$(echo "$RESULT" | grep -oP '\d+ passed' | grep -oP '\d+')
FAIL_NOW=$(echo "$RESULT" | grep -oP '\d+ failed' | grep -oP '\d+')

# Compare with baseline from script injection
# If PASS_NOW < BASELINE_PASSED or FAIL_NOW > BASELINE_FAILED:
#   git checkout .
#   echo "REGRESSION DETECTED — reverting"
#   DO NOT COMMIT
```

The baseline numbers are in the script output injected at the top of your context.

## COMMIT FORMAT

```
fix(tests): make test_X pass — tests 3865 passed (was 3860), 311 failed (was 316)
feat(STORY-NNN): description — tests 3860 passed, lint clean
refactor: description — tests unchanged, lint clean
```

Always include test counts in commit messages.

## KEY DIRECTORIES

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/solstein/` | Source code (ONLY edit here + tests/) |
| `tests/unit/` | Unit tests (primary gate) |
| `src/solstein/analytics/constants.py` | Classification thresholds (source of truth) |
| `src/solstein/research/pipeline.py` | Canonical pipeline |
| `src/solstein/domain/models.py` | Domain models |
| `src/solstein/exporters/` | Export pipeline |
| `src/solstein/api/` | FastAPI endpoints |

## PITFALLS

1. NEVER modify `research/graph/` — FROZEN (STORY-255)
2. Tests need `PYTHONPATH=src` and conftest Settings mock
3. Do NOT add empty `__init__.py` to implicit namespace packages
4. The `_retired/` adapters are still imported by tests
5. Classification: Phoenix ≥7.0, Salt 4.0-7.0, Lead ≤4.0
6. Config has duplicate class bodies — Python discards the first
7. Pre-existing test failures are the BASELINE, not your fault — just don't add MORE
8. `agents/resilience.py` uses lazy `__getattr__` — no module-level Settings calls
9. Full test suite takes ~8 min. Use `-x` for quick fail-fast during dev.
10. Install deps if needed: `python3 -m pip install -e ".[dev]" --break-system-packages`
", "path": "software-development/solstein-coder/SKILL.md", "skill_dir": "/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/solstein-coder", "linked_files": {"references": ["references/autonomous-patterns.md"]}, "usage_hint": "To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'", "required_environment_variables": [], "required_commands": [], "missing_required_environment_variables": [], "missing_credential_files": [], "missing_required_commands": [], "setup_needed": false, "setup_skipped": false, "readiness_status": "available"}

{"action": "patch", "name": "hermes-full-upgrade", "old_string": "## Pitfalls

1. **`redact_secrets: true` hides config values from Hermes tools** — when `security.redact_secrets: true`, reading config.yaml or .env via Hermes tools (`read_file`, `cat`, `grep`) returns `***` instead of real values. Writing `.env` or paste files via `patch()` stores literal `***` strings because the tool filters the output. This also affects `pastes/` files — pasted Telegram tokens get the actual token characters replaced with `***` on disk. Workaround: disable redaction temporarily (`hermes config set security.redact_secrets false`), or use `python3 -c` in `execute_code` to read/write files bypassing the terminal tool. After done, re-enable redaction. **CRITICAL**: Always re-enable redaction after the operation — leaving it off exposes all API keys in plaintext to the agent.", "new_string": "## Pitfalls

### Config & Token Management

1. **`redact_secrets: true` hides config values from Hermes tools** — when `security.redact_secrets: true`, reading config.yaml or .env via Hermes tools (`read_file`, `cat`, `grep`) returns `***` instead of real values. Writing `.env` or paste files via `patch()` stores literal `***` strings because the tool filters the output. This also affects `pastes/` files — pasted Telegram tokens get the actual token characters replaced with `***` on disk. Workaround: disable redaction temporarily (`hermes config set security.redact_secrets false`), or use `python3 -c` in `execute_code` to read/write files bypassing the terminal tool. After done, re-enable redaction. **CRITICAL**: Always re-enable redaction after the operation — leaving it off exposes all API keys in plaintext to the agent.

2. **Systemd user service doesn't source `.env`** — the user-level `hermes-gateway.service` started by `hermes gateway install` has no `EnvironmentFile=` directive. Config values using `${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}` syntax are NOT expanded. The literal string `${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}` gets sent to the Telegram API, fails. **Fix: inline the actual token in config.yaml, or add `EnvironmentFile=%h/.hermes/.env` to the service file.** The system service (`sudo hermes gateway install --system`) includes EnvironmentFile but also runs as root — the user service is cleaner but lacks this feature.

3. **gateway_state.json caches old config values** — when fixing Telegram token from `placeholder` to a real value, the gateway still uses the cached `placeholder` from `/root/.hermes/gateway_state.json`. The health check shows `The token 'placeholder' was rejected by the server` even after config.yaml is correct. **Fix**: edit `gateway_state.json` to update the cached token, or stop the service, delete the state file, and restart.

4. **`hermes cron edit` does NOT accept `--model` or `--provider` flags** — the CLI only supports `--schedule`, `--prompt`, `--name`, `--deliver`, `--repeat`, `--skill`, `--add-skill`, `--remove-skill`, `--clear-skills`, `--script`, `--workdir`. To change the model for cron jobs, edit `/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json` directly using sed or Python:
   ```bash
   sed -i 's|"model": "groq-llama-3.3-70b"|"model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"|g' /root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json
   ```
   Or via Python for complex changes:
   ```python
   import json
   data = json.load(open('/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json'))
   for j in data['jobs']:
       j['model'] = 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4'
   json.dump(data, open('/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json','w'), indent=2)
   ```

5. **WhatsApp bridge exit code -15 causes gateway restart loop** — the WhatsApp bridge process (Node.js Baileys) exits with code -15 (SIGTERM) which triggers systemd `Restart=on-failure`. If the bridge consistently fails (missing credentials, session issues), the gateway keeps restarting in a loop with `status=75/TEMPFAIL`. **Fix options**:
   - Pause any cron jobs that trigger gateway activity during restart (especially IC health checks)
   - Temporarily set `WHATSAPP_ENABLED=false` in `.env` to isolate the issue
   - Remove the WhatsApp bridge process component if it's endlessly looping
   - Check credentials: `grep -i whatsapp /root/.hermes/.env`

6. **Prometheus datasource provisioning breaks with duplicate `isDefault: true`** — Grafana's datasource YAML at `/opt/monitoring/grafana/provisioning/datasources/prometheus.yml` must have exactly ONE datasource marked as `isDefault: true`. If multiple datasources have this flag (or the same one is declared twice), Grafana fails to start with `Datasource provisioning error: datasource.yaml config is invalid. Only one datasource per organization can be marked as default`. **Fix**: remove the `isDefault` flag entirely — Prometheus auto-selects as default when it's the only datasource.

7. **Docker compose `deploy` blocks are ignored by `docker compose`** — the `deploy:` section with `mode: replicated, replicas: 1, placement: constraints` is a Docker Swarm construct. Running via `docker compose up` silently ignores these, which is harmless. But the `ports` mapping under `deploy` is also ignored — **ports must be at the service level, not inside deploy**. If a port change (e.g., `3000:3000` -> `3001:3000`) is applied via sed, the running container won't pick it up until `docker compose down && docker compose up -d`. Use `grep` to verify the compose file after edits."}

```bash
echo 'GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true' >> ~/.hermes/.env

# Restart gateway
```

{"action": "write_file", "file_path": "references/telegram-whatsapp-troubleshooting.md", "name": "hermes-full-upgrade", "content": "# Telegram & WhatsApp Troubleshooting Reference

## Telegram "placeholder" Token Cache Issue

### Symptom
```
Recent gateway health:
  ⚠ telegram: Telegram startup failed: The token `placeholder` was rejected by the server.
```

Even after setting a valid token in config.yaml, the error persists.

### Root Cause
`/root/.hermes/gateway_state.json` caches the old `placeholder` token. The gateway reads this cached value on startup, not config.yaml directly.

### Fix
```python
import json
data = json.load(open('/root/.hermes/gateway_state.json'))
if 'telegram' in data:
    data['telegram']['bot_token'] = 'VALID_TOKEN_HERE'
json.dump(data, open('/root/.hermes/gateway_state.json','w'), indent=2)
```

Also ensure the config.yaml has the token inlined (not as `${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}`) for user systemd services.

### Additional Config Check
Config section should look like:
```yaml
telegram:
  bot_token: [REDACTED_PHONE]:ACTUAL_TOKEN
  reactions: false
  channel_prompts: {}
```

If still failing after both fixes, `hermes gateway stop; sleep 2; hermes gateway start` (not restart, which sends SIGUSR1).

## WhatsApp Bridge Exit Code -15 / Gateway Restart Loop

### Symptom
```
hermes-gateway.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=75/TEMPFAIL
ERROR gateway.platforms.whatsapp: [Whatsapp] WhatsApp bridge process exited unexpectedly (code -15).
[Whatsapp] Bridge ready (status: connected)
[Whatsapp] Disconnected
```

### Root Cause
The WhatsApp bridge (Node.js Baileys process) starts, connects, then disconnects immediately. Exit code -15 = SIGTERM. Systemd sees this as a failure and restarts with `Restart=on-failure`, creating a loop.

### Fix Options

1. **Pause cron jobs that trigger during restart** — IC health checks etc.:
   ```bash
   hermes cron pause JOB_ID
   ```

2. **Fix groq/litellm references in config** — WhatsApp bridge uses the fallback chain. If groq-llama via litellm is in the config and litellm is down, the bridge fails:
   ```bash
   grep -n "groq\|litellm" /root/.hermes/config.yaml
   # Change provider from litellm to openrouter if groq is used
   ```

3. **Temporarily disable WhatsApp** for isolation:
   ```bash
   sed -i 's/WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true/WHATSAPP_ENABLED=false/' /root/.hermes/.env
   ```

4. **Remove caching** — stop, delete state, start:
   ```bash
   hermes gateway stop
   rm -f /root/.hermes/gateway_state.json
   hermes gateway start
   ```

### Gateway State JSON Structure
```json
{
  "telegram": {
    "bot_token": "CACHED_TOKEN"
  },
  "whatsapp": {
    "session": "...",
    "status": "connected"
  },
  "last_shutdown_reason": "...",
  "start_count": N
}
```

## Cron Model Update (Non-CLI)

`hermes cron edit` does NOT support `--model` or `--provider`. Must edit `cron/jobs.json` directly.

### Batch Update All Jobs
```bash
sed -i 's|"model": "groq-llama-3.3-70b"|"model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"|g' /root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json
```

### Verify
```bash
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json')); print({j.get('model','?'):1 for j in d['jobs']}.keys())"
```

### Fix jobs with missing model field
```python
import json
data = json.load(open('/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json'))
for j in data['jobs']:
    m = j.get('model')
    if m is None or 'groq' in str(m):
        j['model'] = 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4'
        j['provider'] = 'openrouter'
json.dump(data, open('/root/.hermes/cron/jobs.json','w'), indent=2)
```

## Systemd User Service Environment

The user-level systemd service (`hermes gateway install`) does NOT include `EnvironmentFile=`. This means:

- `${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}` in config.yaml → literal string `${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}` → fails
- `${GITHUB_TOKEN}` in MCP config → same issue
- Must inline actual values in config.yaml

Check the service file:
```bash
cat /root/.config/systemd/user/hermes-gateway.service
# Look for: EnvironmentFile= — if absent, env vars from .env are NOT loaded
```

Workaround: modify the service file after install to add:
```
EnvironmentFile=%h/.hermes/.env
```
Then `systemctl --user daemon-reload`.

## Getting Telegram User ID for Allowlisting

```bash
# Send a message to your bot, then:
curl -s "https://api.telegram.org/bot${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}/getUpdates" | python3 -c "import sys, json; data=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(u['message']['from']['id']) for u in data.get('result',[]) if 'message' in u]" | tail -1
```
"}
